markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify contradictory claims remain probationary/conflicted until resolved with evidence. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: ContradictoryMemoryFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ContradictoryMemoryFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-231 now enforces contradictory KnowledgeClaim handling through PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. Unresolved claim conflicts keep claims conflicted at the Rust API layer and direct-SQL layer; exiting conflicted to accepted or retired requires all conflicts resolved plus a matching knowledge_claim_conflict EventLedger receipt. Trusted fact projection excludes unresolved-conflict claims even if lifecycle state is stale. HBR-STOP-001/HBR-STOP-002 and CX-503R are proven by real PostgreSQL negative fixtures and raw-SQL boundary tests; HBR-VIS-005 is not applicable because this MT changes no GUI surface.

commands.result: RED before trigger/backstop; raw SQL conflict insert left accepted claims trusted. GREEN after 0320 unresolved-state trigger plus trusted projection unresolved-conflict backstop.
PASS; 5/5 including raw-SQL unresolved conflict fixture, trusted-only filter, graph projection, passage evidence, and visual debug payload composition
PASS; 7/7 including MT-231 conflict exit guards, raw SQL wrong-receipt aggregate rejection, unresolved conflicted->accepted rejection, and unresolved conflicted->retired rejection

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_memory_fixtures_tests contradiction_and_resolution_fixtures -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; contradiction fixture resolves with real knowledge_claim_conflict EventLedger receipt, kept claim accepted, discarded claim retired

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_migration_tests scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; 0320 up/down migration applies, rolls back to zero knowledge_* routines, and reapplies exact routine set including MT-231 receipt and unresolved-state guards

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_claims_tests --test knowledge_memory_projection_tests --test knowledge_memory_fixtures_tests --test knowledge_migration_tests --no-run

commands.result: Product commit succeeded; hook did not auto-send review request because commit subject was not an MT auto-relay subject. Explicit adversarial review was run in-session and returned PASS.

evidence.summary: Reviewer initially found blockers around legacy wrong EventLedger aggregate receipts, unresolved conflicted->retired exits, and raw-SQL conflict insertion leaving accepted claims trusted. Each blocker was hardened with tests and code. Final re-review found no blocking issues; non-blocking note was to ensure the new 0320 up/down migrations were included in the product commit, which bd468f5a includes.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-231 requires contradictory KnowledgeClaims to stay conflicted until resolved with a matching EventLedger receipt, proven by real PostgreSQL negative fixtures. Commit bd468f5a delivers this at the migration, Rust API, and trusted-projection layers with real managed-PostgreSQL proof and no placeholders.
findings: migrations/0320_knowledge_claim_conflict_acceptance_guard.sql: real PG triggers/functions enforce that a conflicted claim cannot exit to accepted/retired while any knowledge_claim_conflicts row (claim_id or conflicting_claim_id) is unresolved, and requires a NEW.resolution_receipt_event_id that joins to kernel_event_ledger with aggregate_type='knowledge_claim_conflict' and aggregate_id=conflict_id (lines ~120-205). Also adds unordered-pair unique index and an unresolved-state guard that pushes involved claims back to 'conflicted'.

storage/knowledge.rs:4139-4360: Rust transition_knowledge_claim mirrors the same three checks (unresolved-count, matching-resolved-conflict, aggregate-authority join) for Conflicted->Accepted/Retired; record_knowledge_claim_conflict rejects reverse-order duplicate pairs; resolve path validates receipt aggregate_type/aggregate_id. Binds API writers and raw-SQL writers to identical authority.

knowledge_memory/projection.rs:171-256: build_fact_graph trusted_only now also requires the backing claim lifecycle_state == Accepted and excludes any claim with an unresolved conflict (claim_has_unresolved_conflict), closing the stale-lifecycle-state trusted-fact leak even when claim row state is stale (CX-503R).

tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-66: proof harness is real-PostgreSQL-only (POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL/managed_postgres ensure_running), per-test isolated schema with full migration chain, explicit 'NO SQLite, in-memory, or mock fallback' — absent binaries SKIP, all other failures panic so a broken cluster cannot look green.

tests/knowledge_claims_tests.rs:58-450: negative fixtures assert expect_err for unresolved conflicted->accepted and ->retired at BOTH the Rust API layer and the raw-SQL layer, a wrong/legacy EventLedger-aggregate receipt rejection, and a resolved-path positive case; conflict pair uses contradictory port assertions (5544 vs 5432).

migration round-trip: scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply test plus the real down.sql (restores prior 0200 guard shape) prove 0320 up/down/reapply; no placeholders, todo!, unimplemented!, or *Unavailable variants anywhere in the diff.

Non-blocking aside: spec section header carries an [ADD v02.192] label while current bundle is v02.194; section 2.3.13.11 content is present and aligned in v02.194. HBR-VIS-005 NOT_APPLICABLE is honest (no GUI surface changed in this MT).

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
